summary: Applied Materials sits at the heart of the AI/HBM, advanced-logic gate-all-around, and DRAM compute-memory cycles. FY26-Q1 printed $7.01B revenue (in-line with guide), and Q2 guide of $7.65B (+9% sequential) signals the first acceleration in 4 quarters. Gross margin guide 49.3% is the highest since FY2000. 8/8 in-line revenue guides over the trailing 8 quarters; 98.6% info-adj MCS.
bull: FY26-Q2 revenue guide $7.65B (+9% sequential, +7% YoY) — first sequential acceleration in 4 quarters; signals trough behind us.
Gross margin guide 49.3% — highest since FY2000, driven by mix shift to advanced foundry/logic + DRAM.
CFE e-beam revenue expected to double in CY26 to >$1B; advanced packaging tracking toward $2B run-rate (6x growth from 2023).
Wins at leading-edge gate-all-around (>50% process equipment share); DRAM revenue up 40%+ in FY25 driven by HBM and DDR5.
Net cash on balance sheet (~$1.3B net cash) + ongoing buyback program; capital return supports valuation floor.
8/8 in-line revenue guides — 98.6% info-adj MCS, top-quartile management forecast accuracy across cohort.
bear: China revenue down 7% YoY; export restrictions a $400M annual headwind that began in FY25-Q2 and continues into FY26.
FY25-Q4 revenue dipped sequentially to $6.8B due to China softness + nonlinear leading-edge customer timing — durability through cycle troughs unproven.
Multiple at FY1 P/E 36.2x is modest discount to peer avg 39.4x — but absolute multiple still rich for a cyclical semicap name.
ICAPS (mature-node) demand flat; structural shift away from non-leading-edge could pressure mix.
Stock has rallied from $153 (52w low) to $437 (~+185%); near-term technical extension and 9-month sustained uptrend risk.
Customer concentration (TSMC, Samsung, Intel, SK Hynix, Micron, GFS) — order timing nonlinear and tied to capex cycles.
trigger_up: FY26-Q2 print on 5/15/26 confirms $7.65B guide hit + GM 49.3% with broad-based growth across foundry/logic + DRAM segments.
Advanced packaging revenue confirmed crossing $2B run-rate by FY26 end; CFE e-beam doubles to >$1B in CY26.
China export-restriction headwind annualizes by Q4 FY26 (last comparable Q4 FY25 hit), enabling clean +mid-teens YoY growth in FY27.
Gate-all-around process equipment share confirmed >50% as TSMC N2/A16 + Samsung 2nm ramp begins late CY26.
Multiple re-rates to 40x+ as cycle re-acceleration narrative takes hold — would imply $580+ price target.
trigger_down: FY26-Q2 misses guide (<$7.15B revenue or GM <48%) — would break the perfect 8/8 in-line streak and signal cycle weakness.
Additional China export restrictions in 2026 broadening the $400M headwind to >$700M annual impact.
Hyperscaler capex deceleration cuts HBM demand growth from ~50% in FY25 to <20% in FY26, hitting advanced packaging.
Major customer (TSMC or Samsung) cuts WFE budget by >10% in FY26, slowing leading-edge equipment ramp.
Geopolitical escalation (Taiwan tension) disrupts customer relationships — would compress multiple toward 25x.
bottom_line: AMAT is the cleanest pick in the semicap basket on management-credibility grounds (8/8 in-line revenue guides, 98.6% info-adj MCS) and is now signaling cycle re-acceleration via the FY26-Q2 guide ($7.65B, +9% sequential, GM 49.3%). FY1 P/E of 36.2x is a modest discount to peer average 39.4x. Near-term risks are technical extension (+185% from 52w low) and lingering China headwinds, but FactSet consensus +25% FY1→FY2 sales growth + advanced-packaging/CFE momentum + capital return support a constructive 3-12 month outlook.

quotes.speaker: Brice Hill (CFO)
quotes.text: As a result of the trade controls announced in December and January, we expect to face a headwind to revenue of approximately $400 million in fiscal 2025.

Cyclical semicap industry — wafer fab equipment spending volatile across cycles.
Stock has rallied +185% from 52w low — near-term technical extension risk.
Concentrated customer base (TSMC, Samsung, Intel, SK Hynix, Micron, GFS) — order timing nonlinear.
